full_prompt: Design the roll-cage (space frame) of a single-seat off-road collegiate vehicle. The engine is a commercially available single-cylinder 10 HP (7.46 kW) 4-stroke; the frame must fully enclose the driver within a cage of circular steel tubing. Primary roll-cage members must be AISI 1018 DOM or AISI 4130 Chromoly steel tubing with minimum outside diameter 25.4 mm (1 inch) and minimum wall 3.05 mm (0.120 inch); AISI 4130 permits 1.65 mm (0.065 inch) wall when heat-treated per rulebook. The cage must pass an in-plane side-impact equivalent static load of 5.0 G lateral applied at the side-impact member, a front impact 4.0 G forward load applied at the front-bulkhead tube, a rear impact 4.0 G rearward load, and a roll-over test: the cage must support 6.0 G vertical at the roof node with no plastic yield. The driver template (95th-percentile male torso/helmet) must fit inside the cage with at least 75 mm clearance to all tubes. Suspension attachment points must withstand a 3.5 G bump at the wheel hub (wheel mass approx 8 kg, so 275 N at the hub plus leverage). Primary verification is linear static structural FEA (beam elements for tubes, solid elements at fishmouth joints). A linear buckling eigenvalue check on the cage under the roll-over 6 G case is required with minimum load factor 1.5. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the frame, a material certificate (tube OD/wall/alloy/heat-treat), and a report listing cage mass in kg, max tube stress per case in MPa, max deflection at driver helmet location in mm, and first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design the tubular steel space-frame roll-cage of a single-seat collegiate off-road (Baja-class) vehicle, enclosing a 95th-percentile driver with helmet and mounting a single-cylinder 10 HP engine and suspension pickup points. Use AISI 1018 DOM or 4130 chromoly tubing per the SAE Baja rulebook.

Envelope: cage on the order of 1.5-2 m long x ~1 m wide, built from circular steel tubing with primary members meeting the rulebook minimum OD/wall. Driver template must have 75 mm clearance to all tubes.

Load cases.
- LC1 side-impact: 5.0 g lateral at the side-impact member.
- LC2 front-impact: 4.0 g forward at the front bulkhead.
- LC3 rear-impact: 4.0 g rearward.
- LC4 rollover: 6.0 g vertical at the roof node, first buckling load factor >= 1.5.
- LC5 bump at hub: 3.5 g at an 8 kg wheel (~275 N at the hub plus leverage) at suspension pickup points.

prompt.title: University off-road vehicle roll-cage
prompt.brief: Design an off-road single-seat roll-cage from 25.4 mm OD x 3.05 mm wall 1018 DOM (or 4130 w/ 1.65 mm), survives 4-6 G impact/rollover cases.

load_cases.name: bump_at_hub
load_cases.description: 8 kg wheel at 3.5 g = 275 N at hub
